entry.title: Re: MM_PUR_S4_PR~MODIFY_ITEM Contract for limit missing i...
entry.content:
Hej Veronika,

sorry, I have no solution for your question. Anyway I am highly intereested in your topic since we have the same request for our customer(s). Using the lean service process with enhanced limit position and "Contract for Limit"-Filed is not populating daat from the assigned contract into PR or PO. This means: supplier, payment terms, texts, etc. in the PR/PO are not filled by data from purchasing contract.

@Oscar_DC : Creating a PR/PO in LeanService process with enhanced limit it is only working with free text position (no product number). This is by design since it is a unplanned service. The product number is just assigned in the service entry sheet.

entry.title: SAP BDC Trial - SAP Databricks Demo Login Error
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am using the SAP Business Data Cloud trial environment and I am facing a login issue with the SAP Databricks demo system.

Until yesterday, I was able to access SAP Databricks without any issue. My usual login flow was:

1. Log in to SAP Business Data Cloud.
2. Go to System Landscape.
3. Select the Databricks system.
4. The Databricks page opens.
5. I enter my own email address on the Databricks login page.
6. I select the SSO option and get redirected to SAP.
7. I log in with the following SAP Experience Academy user:
GE313517@sapexperienceacademy.com

This flow worked correctly yesterday. However, today I receive the following error message during login:

"We've encountered an error logging you in. Reference ID: 30203a14-d067-4ae2-ad0f-452184a4a82c"

I have already tried the following troubleshooting steps:

- Opened Databricks again from the SAP BDC System Landscape launch link.
- Tried in an incognito/private browser window.
- Cleared browser cache and cookies.
- Verified that I am using the correct email address.
- Checked the trial validity period. There are still 26 days remaining in my trial.

The issue still persists.

Could this be related to SAP Cloud Identity Services, SSO provisioning, or the SAP Databricks trial tenant configuration?
Is there anything else I should check, or should this Reference ID be investigated by SAP support?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: 1E3: Custom Ordertyp for Kanban with external Procure...
entry.content:
Dear @ZBMA,

I do not see a released in-app extension or Badi that allows us to override the PO document type / number range used by standard Kanban external procurement. SAP provides Kanban extensibility but the relevant Badi Change Before Creation of Replenishment Element is described for changing custom field values before the replenishment element is created, not for replacing the purchasing document type

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/4c7b54ad7f2d41ae99be5370fed66c0b.html

A side-by-side custom process on SAP BTP could theoretically create POs with a dedicated document type via released APIs but that would replace/extend the standard Kanban replenishment logic and would need separate design for status handling, errors, goods receipt, and process ownership. I would not position this as a simple extension workaround

So there is no clean standard Public Edition extension to meet the requirement 1:1. The recommended next step is an SAP incident to confirm the read-only behavior and if confirmed by SAP an enhancement request / SAP Influence item

More details below:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/s4hana-cloud/continuous-influence

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Change of Controlling Area for a LIVE company code.
entry.content:
Hi 

We have a live company code with company code currency as OMR our controlling area currency is INR. It is now giving us errors in reporting mainly for WBS budgeting. 

Now our business has proposed to change the controlling area for this company code; please let us know the steps and the impact analysis which we should refer for this change as we understand this is a massive impact change.

entry.title: Re: How to access custom VBAK fields in BAPI_SALESORDER_C...
entry.content: Yes, it is possible, but only if you enhance BAPE_VBAK and handle the mapping via the BAPI extension interface. The fields created in the Custom Fields app won’t show up automatically and you need to wire them into the BAPI manually.

entry.title: Activate the “No Rolling” flag in T-code SNRO for object RV_BELEG
entry.content: We are trying to activate the “No Rolling” flag in T-code SNRO for object RV_BELEG using client A1 and a transport request. However, we are unable to activate the flag. Kindly assist us with how to proceed with this activity.

entry.title: Re: Displaying issues with Eclipse Modeling Tools
entry.content: Thanks for sharing FPadt!

entry.title: Unable to activate the “No Rolling” flag in T-code SNRO for object RV_BELEG
entry.content: We are trying to activate the “No Rolling” flag in T-code SNRO for object RV_BELEG using client A1 and a transport request. However, we are unable to activate the flag. Kindly assist us with how to proceed with this activity.

entry.title: GLM label - CBGL_MP01 label preview / print issue
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to preview a label from the Manual Workbench label tree, but it is displaying the raw WWI template instead of the actual data.

However, the label preview works correctly when checked from transactions CG54 and CG42.

From the functional side, all configurations are completed correctly (Label properties, GV, label stock, etc.). This seems like a possible server-side issue, but I have limited knowledge about the WWI server.

Please guide me on what could be causing this issue and where I should check further.

I have attached both screenshots for your reference.

Thanks in advance,
Sai Kumar P

entry.title: Re: How can I get Account dimension hierarchy in Datasphe...
entry.content: Hi @scontran01 this feature is not available in Datasphere at this time, there is a influence request for SAP to work on this item. https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/371610 as a work around it can be achieved with structure members with a restriction on hierarchy node value.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales Process - SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi Durga Prasad, 

Thanks for sharing Advanced intercompany sales process. please share the process flow link.

Thanks 

Ganesh Putla

entry.title: Re: Update time account Using Integration center
entry.content:
Hello,
Make time account detail -- time account external code as calculated field. Then create calculation as "field value = external code of time account & set field association to particular time account type which you want."


Thanks,
Dhanashree

entry.title: Re: Problems with Shopify Syncronization with SAP Busines...
entry.content: This happens when there are trigger and mapping gaps. Since manual sync works, the automatic trigger is likely not firing, so check the scheduler or event setup. The variant issue occurs because the system is picking the product (master) ID instead of the variant ID so mapping should use the variant SKU or ID and treat each variant as a separate item. The overwrite issue happens because the same cross reference is reused, so ensure each item has a unique value like ItemCode or SKU. Fix the trigger and mapping, and it should work correctly.

entry.title: Re: Limit of fields added in the SF to smartrecruiters data mapping
entry.content: Hi, just to clarify, are you saying there is a limit that you have encountered when using the sync tool? Did you receive a specific error message noting the limit?

entry.title: Re: Problems with Shopify Syncronization with SAP Busines...
entry.content: This usually comes down to trigger and mapping issues rather than a system error If manual sync works but automatic does not, the trigger likely is not firing, so check the scheduler or event setup. The variant issue happens when the system uses the product (master) ID instead of the variant ID so mapping should use the variant SKU or ID and treat each variant as a separate item. The overwrite issue occurs when the same cross reference is reused, so each item must have a unique value like ItemCode or SKU. Fix the trigger and mapping and the sync should behave correctly.

entry.title: Re: Process Automation - Authorizations for end users
entry.content: Hello, is there already a solution to this to restrict access to specific processes? IMO using the method with the launchpad if the user somehow gets the link to the form he can anyways use it.

entry.title: Re: Synchronization EBOM and MBOM in S/4 HANA
entry.content: EBOM and MBOM sync in S4HANA is controlled, not automatic. EBOM comes from engineering while MBOM is structured for manufacturing so they are usually different. The sync happens through defined mapping and change management often with PLM integration. If there are issues its usually due to missing mapping rules or incorrect change number handling. Keep a clear transformation process from EBOM to MBOM and manage changes properly to keep both aligned.

entry.title: NEW! Enterprise management layer for SAP S/4HANA - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS01
entry.content:
On Monday May 3rd, 2026, the enterprise management layer team has released its latest template system based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FSP01.

The enterprise management layer is not to be confused with model company for SAP Business Suite.

  • The focus of the enterprise management layer template is large multinational organizations operating in many countries around the world. It provides the foundation of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition by covering all Line of Business. It is by design industry agnostic, so that it can easily be used in all the industries covered by SAP S/4HANA.
  • Model company for SAP Business Suite focuses on the industry-specific content and is available for Germany and the United States.

Some of the Model company for SAP Business Suite is compatible and can be applied on top of the enterprise management layer.

The new release of the enterprise management layer is based on a selected number of scope items (220+) from SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and specific content only available in the enterprise management layer. It covers up to 49 countries; 43 available through SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and 6 only available through the content built by the enterprise management layer team. The system is delivered with 25 languages and is fully Fiori enabled.

The offering is split under in three groups as depicted below:

  • Standard Scope: activation of SAP Best Practices scope items that do not require a specific SAP license other than the core SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Scope Options: activation of SAP Best Practices scope items that require specific SAP license
  • Service Options: activation of content only available in the enterprise management layer

Compared to the previous versions of the enterprise management layer, besides the fact that the content deployed by the activation of SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is based on the updated 2025 FPS01 content, the following specifics shall be mentioned.

Removed scope items:

  • Extended Warehouse Management (Embedded) 
    • WM05D - Synchronous Goods Receipts in EWM  For External Procurement
  • Group Reporting
    • GR02 - Group Reporting - Consolidation of Investments - Actuals Activity-Based 

Updated localization content:

CountryFeatureDescription
AELegal Identifiers for eDocuments exchangeIn preparation to the exchange of electronic documents via the PEPPOL net-work, you can maintain Legal Identifiers for your organization and your Business Partners
ATBalance of Payments – Cross-Border Trade in ServicesThe Balance of Payments - Cross-Border Trade in Services report (AT_BOP_L1) has been updated to support a preview and edit function before submission. You can also run the report annually and in a correction mode if required.
BESelf-billing eDocumentYou can now generate electronic documents for self-billing supplier invoices
ESCanary Islands Tax Codes (IGIC and AIEM)UPDATED: updated section on IGIC in the Canary Islands with Tax Codes and SII related configuration available when activating scope item 3SS. Information and solution provided to calculate AIEM
ESTax Register Book to SII for IGIC (Canary Islands)Added a reference to scope item 3SS to support the calculation of IGIC and SII submission for IGIC.
Provisioning of Invoice Type AJ for specific Tax Schema.
Classification of Tax Codes as Capital Goods supported.
INTax Collected at Source (TCS)UPDATED: Section reviewed and updated as well as configuration related section, process description for TCS on scraps including reduced rate or nihil tax rates.
INeInvoice / e-Way Bill integrationThe SAP Document and Reporting Com-pliance solution for e-invoice and e-waybill using SAP Cloud Integration or SAP Integration Suite (PaaS model) is deprecated and replaced by SAP Docu-ment and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.
INITC04 and Subcontracting ChallansForm ITC04 is a declaration submitted by registered principals to report details of inputs and capital goods sent to or received from job workers (subcontractors) during a specified tax period.
It tracks the movement of inputs and capital goods between a principal and job workers and ensure compliance to the requirements for availing Input Tax Credit (ITC).
ITeInvoiceUpdated: All customer invoices for Business to Business (B2B) and Business to Government (B2G) Business Partners must be sent electronically through Sistema Di Interscambio (SDI)
KRBusiness PlaceUPDATED: the Business Place can now be entered in the field Branch Number in the Header section of the Post General Journal Entries App.
LUPEPPOL Party Type ID LU:MATNew Party Type ID LU:MAT available
 Registre du Commerce et des Societes Number (RCS)You can maintain the Registre du Commerce et des Societes Number (RCS) for your organization
MXDIOT Format 54 fieldsFrom January 2025, the former DIOT Report Format with 25 fields (Report ID MX_VAT_MONTHLY_V0_LOG) is not accepted by SAT and the report with Format with 54 fields (Report ID MX_VAT_DIOT_V0) is required. This report provided a more granular view of the VAT reported and the Suppliers
MYSales and Service Tax (SST-02) ReportFrom September 2025, a new Form SST-02 is required by the Authorities and additional Tax Codes and Tax Boxes mapping required
MYConsolidating invoices over thresholdIf you try to consolidate electronic invoices that are over the threshold of RM 10,000, you get an error message
MYConsolidated Invoice for Buyer-Issued Electronic documentsYou can consolidate Buyer-Issued Electronic documents
MYHandling of Multiple Import Shipments Under a Single Supplier InvoiceWhen importing goods from foreign suppliers, it is possible to generate Buyer-Issued electronic documents for the various goods receipt, and these contain the Customs Form Reference Number
NOParallelization Feature Enabled for SAF-TIf you have large volumes of data to report you can activate parallelization to reduce the time to generate the report
PHTaxpayer Identification Number A number assigned to companies operating in the Philippines after they have registered with the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR).
UPDATED: added section about how to maintain the TIN for domestic and foreign Business Partners and how it displays in Payment Receipts and other reports such as Computer Accounting System and Summary Lists reports
PLe-Invoices (KSeF)UPDATED: Electronic Document Processing (B2B): you can create electronic customer invoices for Poland (also called KSeF invoices) and submit them to the tax authority.
Business to Consumer (B2C) invoices/credit memos are excluded from the electronic document generation.
Schema version FA(3) is available.
PLSAF-T JPK-VAT Monthly/QuarterlyUPDATED: You can now use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance to generate the JPK_VAT XML file on a monthly/quarterly basis. The submission is automatic and done through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.
PLSAF-T JPK_KR_PD YearlyUPDATED: You can now use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance to generate the JPK-KR-PD XML file on a yearly basis. The submission is automatic and done through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.
PLSAF-T JPK_ST_KR YearlyUPDATED: You can now use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance to generate the JPK-ST-KR XML file on a yearly basis. The submission is automatic and done through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.
PLSAF-T JPK_WBUPDATED: The SAF-T Bank Statements report (PL_SAFT_WB) enables you to generate the report on bank account transactions in an XML file format. You can now use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance to generate the report. The submission is automatic and done through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.
PLSAF-T JPK_FAYou can now generate the SAF-T JPK FA report to provide a list of all customer invoices and credit memos. You can now use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance to generate the report. The submission is automatic and done through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.
PLGrouping Payments based on KSEF Reference NumberWhen you create a payment proposal, the system identifies the KSeF (Krajowy System e-Faktur) reference number stored in the invoices. It then groups items with the same KSeF reference number and combines them into a single payment.
PLSAF-T JPK-MAG (Goods Movements) reportYou can now generate the SAF-T JPK MAG report to provide a list of all external and internal goods movements on an ad-hoc basis. You can now use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance to generate the report. The submission is automatic and done through SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.
PTPayment FormatsPayment Formats PT_CGI_XML_CT_V9 and PT_CGI_XML_DD_V8 are available
ROInventory RegisterAs an alternative to the process described under scope item J58_RO Accounting and Financial Close, you can now generate the Inventory Register reports (a.k.a. Registrul-inventar a.k.a. form 14-1-2) using an Ad-hoc report in Document Reporting and Compliance. There are three reports you can generate in that context:
• Inventory Register - Before Inventory (a.k.a Registrul-inventar -inainte inventariere)
• Inventory Register - During Inventory (a.k.a. Registrul-inventar – in curs inventariere); in addition to all accounting entries, this report separately identifies physical inventory adjustment entries, foreign currency valuation adjustment entries and inflation adjustment entries.
• Inventory Register - After Inventory (a.k.a. Registrul-inventar -dupa inventariere)
SGRevenue Adjustment for GST Return and GST Return SubmissionYou can decide which tax items are included or excluded from the revenue calculation reported in Tax Box 13 - Revenue for the accounting period of the Singapore GST Return report (SG_VAT_DCL) and the Singapore GST Return Submission report (SG_VAT_F5)
SGSubmission of electronic invoice to IRASIf your organization is required to do so, you can now submit your electronic documents to IRAS.
THPP36 VAT ReturnYou can now used Deferred Tax Codes when posting import of services from foreign suppliers’ invoices. In addition, Withholding Tax Income Types are used to populate the Payment For section in the Thai PDF form.
TRInput VAT DeclarationThe Input Vat Declaration has been updated and contains three additional columns

entry.title: Get BUT000 data in BDT Event DSAVB
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm using the Business Data Toolset (BDT) for BP to set a specific value in one field of BUT000 table when a specific BP Grouping is used.
I implemented this logic in a custom function module and linked it to the event DSAVB.
Because the function module is not included into the standard environment I need to read the current BUT000 work area.
I tried to use the function BUP_BUPA_BUT000_GET, but its usage generates an infinite loop, because it, in turns , calls the event DSAVB.
I know that there is still the old fashioned way to pull the BUT000 value looking at the calling stack level, but I'm wondering if there is a more modern way to accomplish the task.

Thanks,
Antonino

entry.title: Re: Update SODI e-mail
entry.content:
Hello,

not sure if you got the solution already, but if not try T-Code SADP.  At the selection screen enter SODI into the field with asterix and the other field blank. Usually at the Last Name enter your email adress you would like to change. click on button "Find".  There you should be able to change it.

Best Regards

entry.title: SAC-Datasphere Location Dimension Issue at Choroplet map
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I faced issue in our TDD system with Location Dimension. I created Dimension view and created calculated column as Geo-Dimension with latitude and longitude in Datasphere. I did all process to use this dimension in SAP Analytics Cloud. Location dimension is working fine for Bubble and Heat Map but when i tried to see at Choroplet map feauture i get error message as below: 

i worked with same dataset in other system with same process and it works fine for all map features but in our SAC system it's not working. What can be the issue?

entry.title: Re: CREATE AND ASSIGN AUTHORIZATION POLICIES IN IAS
entry.content:
Hello @ajitchirania88 ,

Thanks for detailed document on the authorization policies. This tab is no longer available in SAP CIS tenant , has anything recently changed in the tenant or there is new process to manage the policies?

entry.title: SAP Business Network: Supporting suppliers through the new UI experience (SBN on BTP)
entry.content:
As SAP Business Network suppliers and buyers begin to experience the new UI—often referred to as NextGen or SAP Business Network on SAP BTP—we’re hearing a clear ask: What’s changing, what’s not, and how do I prepare?

Our goal is simple: help suppliers' awareness and readiness and help buyers support their suppliers through this transition.

To respond quickly and effectively, we started a dedicated webinar series to walk through the new supplier experience, via a live demo, and answer real supplier questions live.

The first session already took place, presented by @BenjiT.  If you couldn’t attend, here’s a quick snapshot of the key takeaways to get you started:

What is not changing?

Suppliers can rest assured that this transition does not impact the foundation of their account:

  • Account type (Standard, Enterprise, Free)
  • Pricing model
  • Existing customer relationships
  • User access, documents, and historical data
  • Supplier configuration.

In short, these remain intact.

What is changing?

This shift is primarily a navigation and user experience change.

Suppliers are gradually moving to a new interface designed to improve supplier experience, usability, adoption and buyer-supplier collaboration on the Network. While the concepts remain familiar, many menus and features are presented differently.

The rollout for existing supplier accounts happens gradually between April and December.  Whilst exact dates cannot be shared in advance, suppliers will receive an official notification before their account transitions.  New accounts already started being created via Walk-up registration since December 2025 . 

What should suppliers do next?

Whether you are a supplier preparing for the change—or a buyer helping suppliers through it—these two steps are the best place to start:

  1. Visit the SAP Business Network for Suppliers Community page
    For the latest updates, guidance, and resources related to the new UI.
    👉Check the top section for the newest information.
  2. Watch the 30‑minute webinar replay
    👉 Get a guided tour of the new supplier experience, hear live questions answered, and review the supporting materials shared during the session.

Watch out for future webinar sessions and/or feel free to leave comments/questions on the Community with our tag ‘SAP Business Network for Suppliers’.

More to come as the rollout progresses!

entry.title: Re: Blog 3 - From BW 7.5 to SAP BDC: Reusing Extractors, Custom Logic, and Models Standard Extracto
entry.content:
Hello @MajidB ,

sorry to say that, but all your included screenshots are veeery small, even you click on enlarge. This means I hardly can recognize the details in it 😞

Do you think it is possible to update the blog with higher resolution screenshots?

Btw. I'm interested in the exact process of the Delta Initialization of a 2LIS extractor. Usually in BW you have the option to load Init with and without data, full and delta. In your screenshot I cannot recognize the options.

Usually in a replication flow with CDS view, you can only load full OR init and delta... how does it work with 2LIS here?

Thanks a lot, Martin

entry.title: Re: CDS View for Pricing Condition Tables in S4 Public Cloud
entry.content: I found the CDS view I_PricingConditionRecord, but it is not released. Could you please release this view? It would be very helpful.

entry.title: Not able to create custom branded client with SSAM 2511 metadata.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm trying to build a custom branded client with 2511 metadata. But, I'm facing an issue while creating the client.

"BUILD FAILED in 28s
Failed to build plugin @nativescript-community/ui-material-core :
Error: Command gradlew.bat failed with exit code 1."

Could you please help me in resolving the issue.

Metadata version - ASSETMGRMD251100P_1-70008962.

SSAM version - 2511.

Below attaching the screenshot for your reference.

Thanks,

Battula Sushma.

entry.title: CPI keypair as expired soon we are trying to update the keypair
entry.content:
CPI keypair as expired soon we are trying to update the keypair.we have the keypair  provided by the client in the form of XML format but xml format was not been supported in the CPI we need JKS format to upload the keypair

Interface name Single Touch Payroll (STP) reporting - Australia

please refer 2705107 - Recommendation on generation of KeyStore (JKS) file for SCPI

entry.title: Re: Super Admin Access
entry.content:
Hi @ahounsome ,

Here comes some explanation for your consideration on the administrator management. 

A super administrator is a privileged user responsible for setting up the instance or inviting others to do so. The first user created after instance provisioning is automatically granted super administrator rights. 

Based on this definition, super administrators will be granted permissions accordingly. We adhere to a minimal privilege policy while ensuring a smooth customer onboarding experience, reducing manual steps and accommodating users with limited SuccessFactors knowledge.

  • access to your SF system
  • access Manage Provisioning Access page to invite and send approval note to Partner for their access to Provisioning system
  • access Manage RBP Admin Access page to grant himself or others to setup RBP.  (User search is a pre-q for adding new admin)
  • access Manage Super Admin Access page to grant others to take the administration work. (User search is a pre-q for adding new admin)
  • access Learning Admin console to setup Learning if Learning module is enabled.

The Admin Overseer, formerly the RBP Super Admin, is a restricted admin role dedicated to admin user management, with no operational responsibilities, and access to Manage RBP Admin Access and Manage Super Admin Access only after instance is setup.

Regards,

Fannie Guan




Should background technical users be set-up as Super Admins? Should our IT administrators be defined as Super Admins rather than Admin Overseers? What is the rationale for including Provisioning and Learning Administration access as part of Super Admin access, given that additional permissions are still required to manage most functional areas of the system?

entry.title: Re: Best Practice Implementation of the SAP Geographical Enablement Framework - Initial Setup
entry.content: Updated to mention the issue with the view variant for GEF_GEOM_3857 prefix. Initial variant had the view name GEOMGEOM which was incorrect. Note 3736310 - Generated views for GEF_GEOM_3857 with incorrect names is now released to correct this to view name GEOM.

entry.title: Re: Agentic coding: does it really work, or is it just a skill issue on my side?
entry.content:
🙂I had been looking for a resource like this for a while, had mainly done Youtube videos and documentation in the past: 

Agentic Coding with Claude Code - Eden Marco - Packt
https://www.packtpub.com/en-us/product/agentic-coding-with-claude-code-9781806022588

 

So far liking it, mainly because you get to see what is behind different pieces in Claude Code.

entry.title: At what point does ERP complexity start affecting business performance?
entry.content: When does ERP complexity stop being manageable and start becoming a real problem for the business? When do you actually start feeling it in daily operations, like things getting slower or more annoying than they should be?

Thanks in advance

Best Regards,
Henry

entry.title: Meetup - Sapphire Madrid 2026
entry.content:
¡Hola comunidad SAP en Madrid! ¿Qué les parece si nos juntamos la noche despues del segundo dia de SAP Sapphire Madrid 2026? La idea es reunirnos para hacer networking, compartir lo que se ha anunciado como parte de SAP Sapphire. Comida y bebida a cargo de cada uno.

El evento sera en Taproom Madrid dentro de Tierra Burrito Bar, C. de San Germán, 6, Tetuán, 28020 Madrid. Por favor confirmar la asistencia con la funcionalidad de RSVP de esta pagina.

------

Hola SAP Community in Madrid! Join us the evening after the second day of Sapphire 2026 in Madrid for an informal dinner and networking session. Great chance to meet SAP Community member and exchange what we've learnt as part of Sapphire. Each person covers their own food and drinks.

The location is Taproom Madrid, dentro de Tierra Burrito Bar, C. de San Germán, 6, Tetuán, 28020 Madrid. Please confirm that you will be attending by using the RSVP functionality on this web page.

entry.title: Re: Error in Budget Uploading through Standard Import Financial Plan Data app
entry.content: Thanks very much for your question. It's been forwarded to experts to advise.

entry.title: Re: I want to restrict the edit functionality after the invoice document posted through workflow.
entry.content: Please provide further context so that the appropriate experts can answer the question.

entry.title: SAP Runs SAP at Sapphire 2026: How SAP Runs Its Own Enterprise
entry.content:
At Sapphire 2026, SAP runs SAP focuses on a straightforward question:
what can be learned from how SAP operates its own business using its own technology?

Across Orlando and Madrid, the SAP runs SAP track brings together more than 50 sessions with over 16 hours of content, supported by SAP’s internal IT experts who are directly involved in running these systems. The intent is not to present a vision, but to provide visibility into how agents, applications, and data are applied in a real enterprise environment.

We offer dedicated sessions tailored to specific buying centers—including COO, CIO, CFO, CHRO, and CRO audiences. These sessions reflect the real challenges SAP is facing and demonstrate how we are using our own technology and solutions to achieve better outcomes. Across finance, HR, procurement, CX, and operations, the emphasis is consistently on how things work in practice: how decisions are supported by data, how workflows are structured, and how AI is embedded into day-to-day operations.

What differentiates the experience is how these sessions extend beyond the stage into direct interaction at the SAP runs SAP Experience Hub.

The Expert Bar provides a direct way to continue the conversation. After a session, or independently, participants can speak with SAP experts, ask follow-up questions, and discuss how specific topics translate into their own context.

The C-suite Experience offers a more structured perspective. It allows visitors to explore how SAP approaches transformation from different executive viewpoints, such as finance, HR, or operations, linking individual decisions to broader business outcomes.

The SAP runs SAP Theater complements this with Ask the Expert sessions that revisit key topics in a more focused setting.

Together, these elements are designed to make it easier to move between listening, discussing, and exploring. Instead of a single entry point, the experience allows visitors to engage with the material at different levels, depending on their interests and priorities.

SAP runs SAP does not focus on what could be done, but on what is already in place and what can be learned from it. For leaders looking for real-world proof, measurable outcomes, and practical lessons, it offers a more concrete view of how AI and enterprise systems come together in practice, and how these insights can be applied in their own organizations.

SAP Sapphire 2026 offers a chance to explore this firsthand—and to engage directly with the people and experiences behind it. To explore SAP runs SAP sessions in more detail, please refer to the session catalogs:

Orlando and Madrid

Get a first exciting taste of what’s ahead—watch our short YouTube teaser videos and get inspired by the insights and perspectives from our SAP runs SAP speakers.

Sapphire is one moment in time, but the discussion continues beyond the event.

The Inside SAP IT YouTube Live series offers a more in-depth look at how SAP operates its IT landscape, with a focus on concrete decisions and implementation topics.

Join the upcoming session here:

entry.title: Calculation error in 'Perform Preference Determination' in GTS E4H
entry.content:
Getting Calculation error in 'Perform Preference Determination' in GTS E4H. Below are the Steps:

Use App - 'Perform Preference Determination' and run the Preference Determination for a products. Some cases, we are getting Calculation errors as attached. Request your urgent to fix such cases. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Intercompany billing (IGA) account assignment
entry.content: we are facing the same issue here, is there any solution to share?

entry.title: Re: Enabling Multiple Instances (Bulk) processing on a custom Migration Object
entry.content: Hi I am trying the same for a custom object did it work? I tried adding the sif_product object as well but the global data is not generated properly receiving error for _var_plv_active_p.Any workaround for this?

entry.title: Re: How has RISE with SAP changed ownership across teams...
entry.content: Yeah, that’s a good way to put it. With RISE with SAP it really feels like Basis doesn’t disappear, it just shifts focus. Instead of owning the full stack, the role becomes more about coordination, understanding what SAP controls vs what the business still owns, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks. That “bridge” you mention is probably the biggest change. I also agree it’s not necessarily simpler, just a different model that forces teams to think more in terms of orchestration than execution.

entry.title: Re: Week 4: AI Developer Challenge SAP-RPT-1 Model
entry.content: User ID: GE308630

entry.title: Assignment of Existing Sales Group to Another Sales Office in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Support Team,

I would like to request guidance regarding the assignment of a Sales Group in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Currently, a Sales Group is already assigned to a specific Sales Office. We need to understand how to assign this same Sales Group to an additional Sales Office.

From our analysis in CBC, it appears that once a Sales Group is assigned to a Sales Office, no further changes or reassignments are possible. 

regards

Daniele

entry.title: Re: Meetup - Sapphire Madrid 2026
entry.content: Trying my best to be there.

entry.title: I cant declare transport error as repaired in SOLMAN (charm)
entry.content: I need to declare an error as repaired in SAP Solution Manager, but when I follow the step-by-step, the option "Declare Error as Repaired" is grayed out and I can’t click it

entry.title: Re: Demo Scenario: Clean Core Extensibility with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build
entry.content:
@Jelena_Perfiljeva Thanks for the detailed feedback

1. The clean core extensibility whitepaper mentioned in the pre-read covers this in page 7. Or do you mean the arguments presented there are not convincing on business value? Would love to understand your take on clean core extensibility with reasoning.

2. Do consider the cost of upgrade projects with delays/budget overshots, bottlenecks to adopt new technology/innovations among others in the "before" solution

3. Yes, you are right on the decision needing more information. This is intended as a demo/sample only. Consider the application extension methodology for actual decision making

4. Sure, cost of services being weighed against the potential benefit in the specific customer context is fair. Scale is one aspect, there could be more. Nevertheless, the intent is to show what is possible. 

5. Thanks for sharing. As stated under assumptions, we here assume no fit-to-standard solution exists (although this may or may not be factually correct)

6. Thanks for the idea. However, there are different categories and segments of readers of such blog posts. On one hand, there are experts like yourselves who've been around and worked on stuff for 20 years, keeping track of the changing SAP landscape. On the other hand, there are others who are trying to grasp the idea of clean core extensibility and how this is different to the classic way of working. Yes, SAP has been talking about clean core for a while now but such high-level examples serve the audience that still don't understand the big picture. 

In general, I like your idea of having a more detailed version talking about architectural/cost trade-offs with a real customer example but unless we can name the customer to gain credibility for such a scenario, there would always be more factors one could bring up for consideration.

entry.title: Re: The Weigh button is not visible in the Component Cons...

Hi,

Currently, Weigh & Dispense is not part of POD 2.0 GA scope. Something similar can be implemented using POD 2.0 capabilities, extensions and custom developments, and APIs. For any W&D in POD 2.0 requirements, please create Customer Influence Portal entry.

Hope it helps! Br, Manoel

entry.title: Group VAT configuration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m working on configuring Group VAT reporting for Hungary in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and would like to understand the required setup steps.

How is Group VAT typically configured for Hungary, especially regarding statutory reporting and NAV XML submission?

Any guidance or best practices would be highly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Joule Skill Call Action: auto-generated branch conditions crash
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I want to report a bug in Joule Studio's Skill Builder that occurs recently when adding a Call Action step to a Joule Skill.

**Environment**
- SAP Joule Studio (Skill Builder)

**Steps to Reproduce**
1. Create an Action Project 
2. Release and publish the action
3. In Joule Studio, create a Joule Skill and add a Call Action step referencing the above action
4. Observe the auto-generated branches on the canvas

**What Happens**
When the Call Action step is added, two branches are automatically generated: Success and Default error. Both branches contain an auto-generated condition expression that references `body.output._statusCode`. 

Since the action has no statusCode output field,  the expression fails immediately with:

`EL1008E: Property or field 'default' cannot be found on object of type 'Map' - maybe not public or not valid?` 

Set variables action executed with errors due to invalid scripting expression

**Impact**
- The skill crashes on BOTH the Success and Default Error branches
- The branch conditions are not editable in the canvas, so there is no way to manually fix the expression
- The skill is completely unusable

**Expected Behavior**
Branches should either:
- Not be auto-generated, OR
- Use a safe default expression 

Is this a a new update? Is there a fix or official workaround planned?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Call for ALM Partners Q2/2026
entry.content: Will this event covers SAP CALM Operations ?

entry.title: Re: Unexpected plugin behaviour of Data collection plugin...
entry.content:
Hi, see some comments below:

1: 'Postings' button will show the previous collected values (your item #3), but it´s not yet fully implemented and has been hidden until fully implemented.

2: That seems WaD (work as design), as it gives you some events where you can attach logic, even if data has already been collected. You can suggest any improvements you see fit anytime via Customer Influence Portal.

3: Will be addressed in standard by 'Postings' button (item #1).

4: That seems a bug, that will most probably be fixed before PROD Release / GA of 2605, but I highly encourage you to raise a ticket for further investigation and proper support.

Hope it helps! Br, Manoel

entry.title: Re: Powering SAP Cloud Transformation with Advanced Data Migration and Continuous Testing
entry.content:
@RACHID_AITBOUALE 

In addition, the following was also recommended.

The recommendation for this type of integration is to avoid letting the AI agent call SAP directly or without control.... It should go through a controlled layer such as SAP BTP, SAP Integration Suite, API Management, or another governed middleware layer.

 From a testing point of view, the key recommendation is to test the API contracts with Tosca API Testing. This includes checking schemas, required fields, permissions, error handling, idempotency, logs, usage limits, and general API behavior.

+1 for Linux support

greetings

entry.title: Adding a Decomposition Tree to SAP Analytics Cloud with D3.js
entry.content:
A practical walk-through of building a Power BI-style decomposition tree as an SAP Analytics Cloud custom widget. We will take it from a blank canvas to a smooth, data-bound, production-feeling visualization. This post is written for SAC analytics developers and anyone who has stared at the custom widget SDK wondering exactly where to start.

Quick side note before we jump in: I used Claude AI to help write the code, which made shipping this significantly faster and saved me from a lot of trial and error!

The Gap in the Market

SAP Analytics Cloud has a highly capable chart catalog. However, if you have ever used Power BI's decomposition tree, you know the one major feature it lacks is a left-to-right hierarchical drill-down. You know the exact interaction: each node has a value bar, you click to expand the next level, and you can instantly answer the question of where a specific number is coming from.

This is easily one of the most loved analytics interactions in the BI space. It is exactly how finance, operations, and revenue teams investigate variance across the board: Total → Region → Country → Customer → Product. In SAC, you can build a tree table or a sunburst, but neither of those options feels quite like that intuitive drill-down.

So, I decided to build one. This post covers the story of how it works, what surprised me along the way, and how you can use the final result.

What We Are Building

The goal was to create a custom widget that delivers on all fronts:

  • Appears in the SAC widget picker just like any built-in chart.

  • Lets designers drag dimensions and a measure into a Builder feed (the standard SAC data-binding user experience).

  • Renders a left-to-right tree starting with a synthetic Total root, followed by one column per dimension level. Each node needs a label, a value, and a horizontal bar sized by the measure.

  • Animates expand and collapse actions smoothly.

  • Survives high-cardinality dimensions (like 10,000 unique customers) without freezing the user's browser.

  • Plays nicely with linked analysis filters originating from other widgets.

                          ┌──────────────┐
                          │  Region      │
                          │  Europe      │
                          │  ███████░░   │
                          └──────────────┘
                          ┌──────────────┐
┌──────────────┐    ┌──→  │  Region      │
│  Total       │    │     │  Americas    │
│  Sales       │ ───┤     │  █████░░░░   │
│  ████████    │    │     └──────────────┘
└──────────────┘    │     ┌──────────────┐
                    └──→  │  Region      │
                          │  Asia        │
                          │  ███░░░░░░   │
                          └──────────────┘

Click Americas and it reveals USA, Canada, and Mexico as a third column. Click any of those and it reveals their respective customers.

A Custom Widget Primer

If you have never built one before, SAC custom widgets are essentially Web Components loaded by SAC directly into your story. Each widget consists of a JSON manifest plus one or more JavaScript files and an icon. The JSON declares the widget's name, properties, methods, events, and optionally a data binding feed configuration. The JavaScript files register custom HTML elements (com-yourname-widget) and implement the lifecycle hooks that SAC calls natively (onCustomWidgetBeforeUpdate, onCustomWidgetAfterUpdate, etc.).

You upload the JSON to SAC, then upload a ZIP file containing the JS files and the icon. SAC hosts these resources and serves them to the browser whenever your widget is placed on a canvas.

Architecture Overview

Three main files handle all of the heavy lifting:

  • decomposition_tree.json: The manifest. This declares properties, methods, events, and the data binding configurations (the dimensions feed and the measure feed).

  • decomposition_tree.js: The main Web Component. This handles the D3 layout, click handling, and transitions.

  • decomposition_tree_styling.js: The Styling panel. This controls the color picker, value scale, Top-N settings, and the default expand depth.

Step 1: The Manifest

The JSON manifest is mostly administrative bookkeeping, but two specific parts are critical.

Web Components

"webcomponents": [
  { "kind": "main",    "tag": "com-example-decomposition-tree",         "url": "/decomposition_tree.js" },
  { "kind": "styling", "tag": "com-example-decomposition-tree-styling", "url": "/decomposition_tree_styling.js" }
]

Using forward-slash prefixed URLs ensures the exact same JSON works whether you do a ZIP upload or choose to self-host.

Data Binding

"dataBindings": {
  "treeData": {
    "feeds": [
      { "id": "dimensions", "type": "dimension",            "description": "Hierarchy levels, outer to inner" },
      { "id": "measure",    "type": "mainStructureMember",  "description": "Bar size and node aggregation" }
    ]
  }
}

This single block does an incredible amount of work for us. It tells SAC to auto-generate the feed-config Builder, wires up the data fetch when designers bind a model, and exposes the final result set to the widget as this.treeData.data and this.treeData.metadata at runtime.

Step 2: From a Flat Result Set to a Tree

When dimensions and a measure are bound, SAC hands the widget a flat array of rows that looks something like this:

[
  { dimensions_0: { id: "...", label: "Americas" },
    dimensions_1: { id: "...", label: "USA" },
    dimensions_2: { id: "...", label: "Acme Corp" },
    measure_0:    { raw: 320000, formatted: "320K", unit: "USD" } },
  // ...
]

To draw a decomposition tree, we must convert this into a hierarchy. The folding process is straightforward. We walk each row, descend through the dimension keys, create child nodes on demand, and accumulate the measure:

const root = { name: "Total", value: 0, children: [], _childMap: {} };

for (const row of rows) {
  const v = Number(row.measure_0?.raw) || 0;
  let cursor = root;
  cursor.value += v;
  for (const dimKey of dimAliases) {
    const label = row[dimKey]?.label || "(empty)";
    let child = cursor._childMap[label];
    if (!child) {
      child = { name: label, value: 0, children: [], _childMap: {} };
      cursor._childMap[label] = child;
      cursor.children.push(child);
    }
    child.value += v;
    cursor = child;
  }
}

That is the entire core algorithm. The rest of the code is just performance and user experience scaffolding built around it.

Step 3: Surviving High Cardinality

The first time I bound a Customer dimension with thousands of distinct values, the browser completely hung. Folding ten thousand rows in memory is fine, but rendering ten thousand SVG groups will destroy performance.

The fix is utilizing a Top N + Others grouping.

At every level of the tree, after sorting the children descending by their measure, keep only the top N values and roll the remainder into a single leaf node labeled "Others (count)".

n.children.sort((a, b) => b.value - a.value);
if (n.children.length > topN) {
  const top  = n.children.slice(0, topN);
  const rest = n.children.slice(topN);
  const othersValue = rest.reduce((s, c) => s + c.value, 0);
  n.children = [...top, {
    name: "Others (" + rest.length + ")",
    value: othersValue,
    _isOthers: true
  }];
}

Step 4: Keeping User State Across Data Refreshes

When a user clicks a bar in a different widget wired up via linked analysis, SAC pushes a new result set to your tree. If you naively recompute the tree, the user's expand state completely vanishes. To fix this, you have to track user actions by path string rather than by node reference.

Every click adds an entry to your tracking sets. After a rebuild, walk the new tree and apply the overrides:

this._root.each(n => {
  const path = pathOf(n);
  if (this._userExpandedPaths.has(path))  this._collapsedNodes.delete(n);
  if (this._userCollapsedPaths.has(path)) this._collapsedNodes.add(n);
});

Step 5: Dimension Chips at Runtime

Story viewers often want to try the tree using different decomposition orders without calling a designer. To solve this, I added a clickable dimension chip bar above the tree. Clicking a chip removes that level from the fold, and clicking a disabled chip adds it right back. The tree naturally re-folds on the fly.

Step 6: Visual Polish

To make this feel like a native, premium product, I added some final touches:

  • A clean system font stack.

  • An 8px corner radius with white nodes on a faint gray background.

  • A 3px blue accent strip on the left edge of nodes that have children, providing a subtle visual affordance that the node can expand.

  • A soft drop-shadow on hover using an SVG filter.

  • A selected-path highlight where clicking a node lights up the specific path from the root directly to that node with blue borders and blue links.

Grab the Code and Try It Out

If you want to dive deeper, check out the full source code, or just grab the files to test this in your own SAP Analytics Cloud tenant, I have put everything up on GitHub : Decomposition Tree 

You can find the full repository here: 

I highly recommend reading through the repository README. It contains the complete configuration table, script API details, and some extra technical notes that did not fit into this post.

How to Upload the Widget to Your SAC Tenant

Getting this running in your own environment only takes a minute or two. You do not need to compile anything. Just follow these steps:

  1. Download the files from the GitHub repository.

  2. Create a ZIP file containing exactly three items: decomposition_tree.js, decomposition_tree_styling.js, and icon.png. Leave the JSON file outside of the ZIP.

  3. Log in to your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant.

  4. Navigate to the Custom Widgets area. You can usually find this by going to the left hand navigation menu, clicking Stories, and selecting the Custom Widgets tab.

  5. Click the + Create icon (or Add button) to upload a new widget.

  6. Upload the manifest first. Select the decomposition_tree.json file from your downloaded folder.

  7. Upload the resources. When SAC prompts you for the resource files, select the ZIP file you created in step two.

Happy decomposing!

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud : Comment for Cross calculation
entry.content: @etienne1110, did you somehow manage to find some workaround for this?

entry.title: How do I expose views from Hana via Service Layer v2?
entry.content:
Question has been asked before, but answers do not apply to my scenario and are generally outdated.

As in subject: I need to expose some of my views through Service Layer. The only method I've currently found is to create wrappers in form of Calculation Views: this approach works, but it's incredibly cumbersome. I really need an easier way, that can possibly be implemented in a setup procedure (e.g. via SQL scripts, DI Api, direct Hana access via ADO.NET or a combination of these methods).

I've already tried using the /b1s/v2/SQLQueries endpoint: unfortunately, this endpoint won't allow the creation of complex queries, seriously limiting its potential; several SQL commands are not supported and the query gets discarded. 

Between Google searches and AI chats I get there are other methods, but I honestly can't wrap my head around this issue. To recap, what I need is:
- creation of several views with a one-off script or unattended program (no GUI, no wizards, no packages and so on): creation is trivial, as it's just a CREATE instruction
- exposition of these views via Service Layer
- interrogation of these views via Service Layer

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Best Practice Implementation of the SAP Geographical Enablement Framework - Initial Setup
entry.content:
Hi Kevin,

Thanks for the update.

Faced the same issue and solved by updating business object and geo object GDB View name as GEOMGEOM.

Regards,

Shirsendu Roy

entry.title: Re: Reuse Crystal reports template from VB to .net4.8 C#

See this sample on using Parameters and other functions, it has sample code on setting the report to a dataset:

How To: Parameters in Crystal Reports for Visual Studio .NET

entry.title: Re: Response Header validation Error in SAP CPI
entry.content: Are making any other call before this? if yes then remove this header

entry.title: Re: Business Calc Amount1/2 - SAP SuccessFactors Variable...
entry.content:
Hi @Joana_Rochete , please take the lead in organizing your own troubleshooting now that I've shown that it is working in a demo environment. It is the nature of Variable Pay that configuration is complex and anything you change anywhere may have an impact on everything else. From the get go when I saw your first message and screenshots I wondered why you didn't respect the format for non-decimal numbers which should always be #,##0 (with a 0 at the end). If I were you, since you are seeing a bit of a breakthrough with the first idea, I would test the following things:

  1. Assign a Money format with format #,##0.##### to these two columns AS WELL AS TO ALL OTHER other standard columns you are using in your configuration. Then rerun bonus calculation and relaunch a new worksheet to see the outcome on the two fields you are interested in.
  2. If you are seeing progress with correct values in the Bus Calc Amount 1 and 2, then slowly adjust your configurations to single out where the issue was until everything is working as expected.

Please don't edit the original post but add new comments with new screenshots in case you still need help.

If it can be of any help, I am attaching the XML from my demo environment.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1_1l3mz9YmvTQ4YdvTr7u0rogJZMNIB6K/view?usp=sharing

Thanks,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Calibration Process Steps
entry.content:
Dear,

Any exemples from Catalogs and Set Selecteds ?

 

Regards,

Adilson

entry.title: Re: Get Artefacts from type REST API from BTP-CI Integrat...
entry.content:
Try below oData API

https://api.sap.com/api/IntegrationContent/resource/Integration_Packages_Design

entry.title: Requirements created in SAP Cloud ALM can now be seen in SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Have you ever asked yourself how the requirements created in Fit to Standard workshop can be shown and used in SAP Signavio? This is now possible thanks to the new capability in the integration between SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM, released this April 30th.  

The Challenge

Previously, Process Modelers faced challenges in accessing process requirements directly within SAP Signavio. They were required to navigate to SAP Cloud ALM to identify applicable requirements for their process models and then return to Signavio to implement those requirements during the modeling process. 

How it Works

With the updated workflow, users can now initiate the creation of requirements directly from SAP Signavio, which seamlessly redirects them to the relevant section in SAP Cloud ALM. After creating and linking the requirements to the project, scope, and the synchronized process model in SAP Cloud ALM, these requirements will automatically appear in SAP Signavio, organized by project and scope. This streamlined integration ensures that all necessary information is conveniently accessible within SAP Signavio, enabling users to manage process modeling and requirements efficiently from a single interface. 

The new feature uses the best of both products. You can continue to create requirements in SAP Cloud ALM within your project, and once you take the following steps, those requirements become directly applicable to your SAP Signavio process models: 

  •  Sync the SAP Signavio process model to SAP Cloud ALM, where it becomes part of a solution process 
  • Publish the solution process in SAP Cloud ALM 
  • The solution process is added to your project's scope which has enabled Custom Solution scenarios and 
  • The requirement is assigned to solution process or its elements 

Regardless of whether your requirement is assigned to a solution process, process flow, process activity or value flow, all related requirements will appear directly in the SAP Signavio diagram page for your process model.   The new Requirements section in the Diagram page in the screenshot below shows requirements grouped by project and scope, providing details of the requirement together with navigation button for more details in SAP Cloud ALM, as well as an opportunity to create requirements in SAP Cloud ALM, by clicking the Create button: 

During the Fit-to-Standard workshop, when requirements are gathered in a scenario where prior assignments have already been completed, the requirements can be conveniently accessed directly beneath the process model. This streamlined approach not only saves time but also centralizes the brainstorming process, fostering efficiency and collaboration.

How to Enable it

Don't want to enable requirements for your workspace? No problem, you can easily opt in and opt out. The following screenshot presents the necessary switch which enables the requirements for your workspace: 

After the requirements are enabled, you need to allow their appearance on the Diagram page, by selecting Requirements in the following page:

Final Thoughts

Regardless if you are Process modeler or Requirements creator in Fit to Standard workshop, the Diagram page now provides you detailed view of requirements related to your process model. Implementing the whole workflow from creating through assignment of requirements and their appearance in SAP Signavio is easy with a step-by-step guide which you can find in our documentation:  

https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-manager/user-guide/viewing-requirements-in-sap-signavio

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign Collection Management Attributes via...
entry.content:
Hi @Chris1973 ,

First of all, thank you for taking the time to go through my messages and for sharing such detailed suggestions—I really appreciate it.

At the moment, we are following the same approach you described: creating/updating customers via API and then assigning the Collection Profile using the Schedule Collections Management Jobs – Mass Changes functionality.

The challenge we’re facing is that these mass change jobs cannot be scheduled dynamically on a daily basis. For example, when we create a job like “Assignment of Collection Profiles to Business Partners” in the Application Jobs app and try to use a parameter such as “Created On,” there is no option to dynamically populate this field based on the job execution date.

Typically, SAP provides a “Dynamic Dates” option for such parameters, but in this case, it’s not available. This makes it difficult to schedule a single recurring job for daily execution.

As a workaround, we are currently creating individual jobs for each day. For instance, for a full month, we create 31 separate jobs, each with the “Created On” date hardcoded to match the respective execution date. Ideally, we would prefer to schedule a single job that runs daily, with the “Created On” field automatically set to “Today” instead of a fixed date.

Please let me know if there’s an alternative approach or workaround we could explore here.

entry.title: Re: Sapces in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @SJSAPIDL_2,

​1. 
​Yes, we have same object in two spaces but when it shows in 2nd space as a shared but with a distinction between the "Source" and the "Target." An object (Table or View) is physically owned by one space (the Source Space), but it can be shared with and utilized by multiple other spaces.


​2. If we share Table-A from Space-1 to Space-2, where does it exist?
​When you share Table-A from Space-1 (Source) to Space-2 (Target), the object remains in both spaces, but they play different roles

When you share an object, you are not creating a second copy of the data. Space-2 is essentially looking at a "pointer" or a virtual reference to the data sitting in Space-1. This ensures a "Single Source of Truth."

Thank You.

entry.title: Re: Introducing Application Vulnerability Report for Cloudfoundry Applications – Try It Now!
entry.content:
Hello @henry_brink,

is it on the roadmap to allow customer to start a scan manually? 

Or will frequency increased by default?

Thanks

Michael

entry.title: Re: Date Override field not recognized in Compensation fo...
entry.content:
Hi @MariaCarlotaAbreu 

Only the examples explained in the blog below have worked for me.

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/dates-and-roundings-in-compensation/ba-p/13461599

Try with example 5 and use the 0 or 1 as an attribute to an additional column with a custom formula. (if value is 1 then bring this field ID, otherwise leave blank).

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign Collection Management Attributes via...
entry.content: Sorry, but I couldn’t find any inbound service with the name “API_UDM_COLLECTIONS_MANAGEMENT.”

entry.title: Is there a way to split PPV when posting a Goods Receipt?
entry.content: I have a client who is creating standard cost estimates for their purchased(raw) materials.  The problem with this is that it generates a PPV when a Goods Receipt is entered against the PO.  I'm aware of splitting production order variances but I was wondering does anyone know a trick on how to split Purchase Price Variances?

entry.content: Hello everyone, we're facing the same issue. Has anyone been able to resolve it? thanks !!!! 🙂

entry.content: Thanks a lot, @JacsonMiranda !

entry.title: Re: Celebrate Your SAP Academic Community Conference experience: Earn Your Exclusive Conference Badg
entry.content: I will be attending SAP ACC LAC. Thank you @kathrindietenberger

entry.title: Re: Introducing Application Vulnerability Report for Cloudfoundry Applications – Try It Now!
entry.content:
Hi @henry_brink ,

That's awesome and shows it's full potential. I've two more technical questions about this:

1) Would you happen to have the relevant "Source Vulnerability ID" for the sap npm issue so we can more easily monitor for them (we've quite some findings in our older apps and it's hard to scroll through them all)
2) How would the scenario play out that a developer has loaded this into his BAS (I guess you can do npm install there too afterall) and thus the issue is within his environment and active, is this something SAP is screening/cleaning? This tool AFAIK only checks the deployed programs, but not whatever someone has running in BAS or VS. Any ideas?

entry.title: Re: WSAEWOULDBLOCK: Resource temporarily Unavailable : Wh...
entry.content:
This is an old post but it comes up as a top result on Google search for this error. Which is still happening in 2026. 🙂

The note mentioned by the legendary Matt Fraser has been removed. I found this new note: 3301728 that explains the error and has a link to another note. If this note gets obsoleted too, just go to the SAP Support portal (whatever its current name is, SAP for Me right now) and just search for WSAEWOULDBLOCK.

If the issue is consistent and not sporadic and your connection details are correct, 99% it's the firewall issue. Contact your network team. Hope this helps someone.

entry.title: Re: Exception handling for actions in SAP BPA
entry.content:
Hello @davide_bramati ,

You can check the official documentation for further details, as it's quite nice, but in summary,  you currently have a two options on handling errors that occur during Action execution:

  1. Retry mechanism - Allows you to retry the call in case of failure with a given interval and number of attempts
  2. Error handling - In case of specific responses 

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Agenda oficial divulgada: confira a programação do SAP Inside Track Recife 2026
entry.content:
🚀A agenda oficial do SAP Inside Track Recife 2026 está no ar!

No dia 23 de maio, a comunidade SAP se reúne em Recife para uma edição muito especial. Em 2026, celebramos 10 anos da SAP Community Recife, uma trajetória construída com colaboração, troca de conhecimento, networking e muita dedicação de profissionais que acreditam no poder da comunidade.

A programação deste ano foi pensada para conectar temas atuais do ecossistema SAP com experiências reais de mercado, passando por SAP S/4HANA, SAP BTP, Business AI, Clean Core, SAP Signavio, Fiori, manutenção digital, jornada Cloud e tendências para o futuro do consultor SAP.

Ao longo do dia, teremos palestras, momentos de networking, coffee breaks, almoço, sorteios e encerramento oficial.

 

Confira a programação:

09:00 – 09:20
Abertura Oficial e Premiação 10 anos SAP Community Recife
Palestrante: Marssel Vilaça

09:20 – 09:50
Por que decidimos ir para o S/4HANA 2025? Desafios da atualização e uso de IA com Joule
Palestrante: Antonio Figueiredo

10:00 – 10:30
A conectividade em SAP Finanças e sua contribuição para a digitalização
Palestrante: Manoel Robalinho

10:30 – 11:00
Coffee Break — hora do networking!

11:00 – 11:30
Quem vai matar sua Z: Clean Core ou a IA?
Palestrante: Leandro Dante

11:40 – 12:10
Business AI começa no funcional: Fundamentos e Desafios da Adoção Real
Palestrante: Thiago Verta Eiras

12:10 – 13:30
Almoço

13:30 – 13:50
Sorteio de brindes pós-almoço, cuidado pra não perder!

13:50 – 14:20
Consultor SAP: Extinção ou Evolução?
Palestrante: Leandro Barbosa

14:30 – 15:00
Jornada da manutenção digital no SAP
Palestrante: Luciano Martins Rodrigues

15:10 – 15:40
Como o Suporte SAP apoia sua Jornada Cloud!
Palestrante: Edson Thomaz

15:40 – 16:10
Coffee Break — hora do networking!

16:10 – 16:40
O Fim do Fiori tradicional: IA, Agentes e Low-Code Reinventando o Desenvolvimento SAP
Palestrante: Gabriel Andrade

16:50 – 17:20
Como utilizar o SAP Signavio para resolver o gap de conhecimento nos processos de negócio
Palestrante: Rogério Toscano

17:20 – 17:50
Tendências SAP
Participação: Deloitte

17:50 – 18:00
Sorteio final + Encerramento oficial

Mais do que uma agenda de palestras, o SAP Inside Track Recife 2026 será um dia para fortalecer conexões, compartilhar experiências práticas e celebrar o crescimento da nossa comunidade.

Agradecemos aos nossos patrocinadores pelo apoio na realização desta edição especial:

Accenture
Capgemini
Neptune
Fusion
Deloitte

📅Data:
23 de maio de 2026
🎟️ Ingressos:
bit.ly/sap-inside-track-recife-ingressos
📍Evento:
bit.ly/sap-inside-track-recife-2026
🔴Ao vivo:
bit.ly/sap-inside-track-youtube

🔗Hashtag oficial:
#SITREC2026

Nos vemos lá para celebrar 10 anos de SAP Community Recife! 🚀

entry.title: Re: How can I get Account dimension hierarchy in Datasphe...

entry.title: Re: Sales Configuration display by CDS with table function/HANA SQL for Embedded Analytics and Fiori
entry.content: Nice blog. Thanks for sharing.

entry.title: Lessons Learned from Hypercare to AMS Transition in S/4HANA Projects
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In several SAP S/4HANA projects, I have observed that the transition from hypercare to AMS support is often more challenging than anticipated, even when KT activities, documentation, and sign-offs are completed successfully.

Systems may appear stable during the early go-live period, but operational issues can surface later during peak business cycles such as month-end processing, interface spikes, or batch job overlaps.

Some areas that I personally found important during AMS transition include:

  • Understanding real production behaviour during peak load
  • Identifying critical monitoring periods
  • Capturing lessons learned from hypercare
  • Enhancing coordination between project and AMS teams
  • Building AMS team troubleshooting confidence before full takeover

In certain situations, temporary workarounds or manual monitoring activities performed during hypercare were not fully documented during KT, which later created operational ambiguity for support teams.

I would like to hear from the community:

  • What are the key challenges you have seen during Hypercare to AMS transition in S/4HANA projects?
  • Are there any best practices your teams follow to improve operational readiness and long-term support stability?

I look forward to hearing different experiences and approaches from the community.

Best regards,
Raghu Garapati

entry.title: Agenda oficial do SAP Inside Track Recife 2026 foi divulgada!
entry.content:
🚀A agenda oficial do SAP Inside Track Recife 2026 foi divulgada! (Post Linkedin)

Agora é oficial: a programação do nosso evento já está disponível na SAP Community Recife.

E este ano tem um motivo ainda mais especial para participar: estamos celebrando 10 anos da SAP Community Recife, uma história construída com conhecimento compartilhado, networking, colaboração e muita energia da comunidade SAP.

Teremos um dia completo com palestras sobre SAP S/4HANA, SAP BTP, Business AI, Clean Core, SAP Signavio, Fiori, jornada Cloud, manutenção digital e tendências para o futuro do ecossistema SAP.

☕Coffee breaks
🎁Sorteios
🤝Networking
🎤Grandes palestrantes
🚀Muito conteúdo SAP

Corre ligeiro para conferir a programação completa no blog da comunidade 👇

📅Data:
23 de maio de 2026


🎟️ Ingressos 📍Evento 🔴Ao vivo

Nos vemos lá!

hashtag#SAPCommunity
hashtag#SAPInsideTrack
hashtag#SITREC2026

entry.title: Re: 🇲🇽 ABAP AI: Capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers CodeJam (Monterrey, Mexico)
entry.content:
Buen día !!!! 

Tenemos un meetup después del CodeJam para convivir de forma más relajada, comer y platicar con los SAP Advocates… y sobre todo con ustedes, que son el corazón de todo esto.
Pueden registrarse y ver la info aquí: SAP Stammtisch Monterrey - CodeJams Latam 2026 - SAP Community

 

We’ll have a meetup after the CodeJam to hang out in a more relaxed setting, grab some food, and chat with SAP Advocates… but most importantly, with all of you—the heart of it all.
You can register and find all the details here: SAP Stammtisch Monterrey - CodeJams Latam 2026 - SAP Community
